sections.content: Life often moves faster than we realize. We carry thoughts of tomorrow, memories of yesterday, and expectations from others, leaving little space to connect with ourselves. True self-love begins in small, mindful ways. It is pausing to breathe, noticing your feelings, and accepting that you are enough as you are. It is learning to treat yourself with the same kindness you offer to others.

Living in the moment is a form of freedom. It is feeling the warmth of the sun on your skin, hearing the quiet rhythm of rain, or enjoying the smile of a friend. These simple experiences are life unfolding, and they matter more than we often recognize. Focusing on now lightens the weight of what ifs and uncertainties. Life is not a race or a series of achievements; it is a collection of moments meant to be felt and appreciated.

Worrying about the future drains the energy of today. Planning is useful, but it should not overshadow the present. Paying attention to your thoughts, acknowledging your feelings without judgment, and practicing self-compassion can transform how you experience life. Every small step of mindfulness helps you feel grounded and calm.

Self-love also means forgiving yourself for mistakes, doubts, and fears. Trusting that you are enough without needing constant validation brings a quiet peace. Celebrating small victories, embracing your journey, and allowing yourself to grow at your own pace make life lighter and more joyful.

Happiness does not come from controlling what lies ahead but from embracing the present. When you love yourself fully and stay present, every moment becomes meaningful. You are enough here and now, and that is more than enough.

essays.title: From Doubt to Determination: My College Journey
essays.author: Kavi

sections.heading: A Beginning to Remember
sections.content: The first day I stepped into my college is still fresh in my memory. It was around 8:30 in the morning, and everyone was rushing to attend the morning prayer. In the middle of that busy scene, I found myself standing quietly in front of the statue of Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam. At that moment, a spark lit inside me: “One day, I too want to become an achiever like him.” That was the moment my college journey truly began.

sections.heading: From Useless to Unlimited Potential
sections.content: Before coming here, I often thought of myself as “useless.” I couldn’t see any special qualities in me. But this college changed everything. It discovered strengths I didn’t know I had. It gave me confidence, courage, and belief in myself. More than just academics, it became the place where I discovered who I really am.

sections.heading: Professors Who Became My Pillars
sections.content: One of the greatest blessings of my college life was the staff. Our professors were not just teachers—they were mentors, friends, and constant supporters. Our HODs were like patient parents, guiding us at every step. And our principal, a strong yet caring leader, always stood for justice and gave us the courage to choose the right path. With such guidance, I never felt alone.

sections.heading: A Campus That Felt Like Home
sections.content: The campus itself was more than just a set of buildings—it was a living, breathing space full of memories. Its peaceful atmosphere, clean surroundings, and quiet corners made me feel safe and at home. Every corner held a story, every spot a lesson.

sections.heading: The Library: My Source of Inspiration
sections.content: Among all the places in college, the library was my true inspiration. The silence, the smell of books, the presence of knowledge—it became my favorite place. Sitting there, I dared to dream bigger. It gave me the hope that I could one day become something great—maybe an IAS or IFS officer.

sections.heading: Discipline That Built My Character
sections.content: Our college followed strict discipline, especially during exams. No unfair practices, no shortcuts—only honesty and hard work. From this, I learned an important truth: success is earned through effort, not tricks. That discipline shaped my character and prepared me for the challenges of life beyond college.

sections.heading: Lessons Beyond the Classroom
sections.content: What I gained here was not limited to textbooks. This college taught me leadership, responsibility, and courage. It gave me wisdom to face life, not just exams. The person I am today—confident, hopeful, and determined—is the direct result of the foundation built here.

sections.heading: A Painful Goodbye, but a Hopeful Tomorrow
sections.content: As my final year comes to an end, my heart grows heavy at the thought of leaving. Yet, I know this is not a permanent goodbye. One day, I will return—not as a student, but as a successful individual. I dream of standing once again in front of that statue of Dr. Kalam—not just as a dreamer, but as an achiever.

essays.title: THE PRESSING NEED FOR CONNECTION AND COMPASSION
essays.author: A. Dinesh Jerry | 25PCP826 | I M.Sc. Counselling Psychology

sections.content: Today’s world has been shrunk by the principles of globalisation. The immense development of modern communication devices has made it possible to see events taking place in one part of the world while we live in another. What is happening is that even though the world is growing and reaching the peak of scientific development, the human mind and human life are in turmoil and restless. The mechanical life of today’s man, the neglect of psychological well-being, substance abuse, economic crisis, etc., have distorted the balanced personality of the human being. Thus, the events of contemporary change bring stress and destroy the hope of future well-being, even leading to personal destruction. Sometimes, stress blunts a person’s knowledge, ability, and attitude to the point of taking their own life.

Depression has become a major problem in our country today. Natural calamities, rapid social changes, and unresolved trauma have pushed many relationships and individuals into states of irreversible stress. Happiness cannot be enjoyed unless the social environment is stable. Every event requires awareness, policies, and attitudes that support collective mental health. Social services, community events, healthy relationships, and friendships help both individuals and communities to thrive. It is a time when people yearn for the vital support of connection. Human beings cannot live in isolation. We are wired to seek support from one another.

Old age is often seen as a lonely, burdensome season in life. Yet, the elderly are a great asset to society. Ageing brings biological changes, including the gradual decline in the function of nerve cells. The psychological well-being of the elderly can be greatly improved when family and community offer emotional, mental, and social support. Their stress and loneliness can be reduced by valuing their opinions and offering love and care, especially for those facing depression.

In today’s world, young people are at the forefront. Youth is not merely a stage of life, but a gift—marked by imagination and creativity. Many young people feel misunderstood and unsupported by society, which leads to distress and confusion. When they are not trusted or heard, they may be misled, and their potential wasted. Family and community support play a crucial role in shaping young people into better citizens.

Peace and unity flourish when we give and receive support. We must recognise those around us struggling with depression. Their lives depend on how we respond—whether we offer light or deepen their darkness. People who suffer from depression should be seen as part of us, not as burdens. Psychological support—through empathy, active listening, positive reinforcement, and non-judgmental presence—is a key to healing. We must help them overcome feelings of inferiority and guilt, and build healthy, respectful relationships.

Let us dedicate ourselves to supporting those who have lost their well-being due to depression. Let us extend our hands not just in sympathy, but in consistent psychological support—bringing them hope, clarity, and a renewed sense of life.

essays.title: What is up with all the ships?
essays.author: Jeni Rooba

sections.content: Love has been the same in its chemistry, but not in its intentions. The term ‘relationship’ has lost its true meaning in this hyped-up generation. The word ‘love’ now needs a prefix—‘true love’—to regain its depth.
New terms like situationship, courtship, sneaky-link-ship, almost relationship, textationship, flirtationship, and more reflect people's fear of commitment and healing.
Instead of healing their traumas, people invent new ‘ships’ to justify emotional hesitation. These labels make chaos feel manageable.
But the phases of love have always been the same—what has changed is our fear of calling it what it is.
We dress our fears in clever words and call it clarity. We rename confusion as choice, pain as progression, and detachment as freedom.

Perhaps it’s not love that has changed, but we who have stopped meaning what we say. Love was never supposed to be this complicated. Maybe we're not redefining it—maybe we're just afraid of truly feeling it.

essays.title: Netflix & Notes: Best Series to Watch Without Failing Exams
essays.author: Sowmiya C

sections.heading: Pop Culture Plug
sections.content: It’s always quite a difficult task to bring together education and entertainment, but then it’s not impossible. Here I’ve got a perfect series recommendation which will not just keep you entertained but helps in your studies as well. It’s the Korean series “Itaewon Class” that I’m talking about.

“Itaewon Class” has got a powerful storyline with resilient characters which makes it more engaging and motivational to the viewer. The protagonist Park Sae-ro-yi, a determined young man overcomes economic struggles, personal issues, injustice and discrimination to open a successful business which he builds from the scratch. Despite repeated failures he keeps trying, moving ahead.

sections.heading: Key features that motivate an individual
sections.content:
• Persistence: Sae-ro-yi’s story shows that consistency and being upright helps an individual move towards their goal.
  • Growth mindset: The protagonist’s tendency of being open to wide range of ideas and learning new things remains a key factor for success of his business.
  • Inclusive nature: Breaking through all stereotypes, the series showcase how people of diverse background and identity work together, thus implanting the idea of inclusivity among the viewers.
  • Faith in oneself: The series sets forth emphasis on how one should trust themselves and in their capability with the example of Sae-ro-yi who is confident in himself though others doubted and discouraged him.
  • Determination: Through the characters, the series brings out how an individual should be strong willed despite various setbacks.

sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.content: Through its emotional and unique way of narration, the series “Itaewon Class” inspires the viewers to fight for their dreams breaking through all the barriers and perceiving setback as a step stone.
